messages.author: meshmanic

messages.author: nutcracker
messages.text: So, been out doing a bit of “WalkDriving” with the dog, testing the radios (handheld companion and my loft repeater). I’ve written a simple echo repeater using the MeshCore CLI Python API that within a private channel, it will echo back any messages I send to it (via another radio in my study). Impressive on the range given the buildings and that in the way. I noticed when out of range (and to be expected!) that messages will have a status of just “Sent” and when in range there’s an indication of the number of repeats heard. But, when I’m back in range, the messages are being received but are echoed as older messages. It’s as if there’s been a delay and for every message I send now, it’s playing catch up and sending echos back of what I sent before - it seems it won’t ever catch up. Is that to be expected OR do I have something wrong with my Python script! ChatGPT seems to imply FIFO is happening here but what are your experiences?
messages.content_html: So, been out doing a bit of “WalkDriving” with the dog, testing the radios (handheld companion and my loft repeater). I’ve written a simple echo repeater using the MeshCore CLI Python API that within a private channel, it will echo back any messages I send to it (via another radio in my study). Impressive on the range given the buildings and that in the way. I noticed when out of range (and to be expected!) that messages will have a status of just “Sent” and when in range there’s an indication of the number of repeats heard. But, when I’m back in range, the messages are being received but are echoed as older messages. It’s as if there’s been a delay and for every message I send now, it’s playing catch up and sending echos back of what I sent before - it seems it won’t ever catch up. Is that to be expected OR do I have something wrong with my Python script! ChatGPT seems to imply FIFO is happening here but what are your experiences?

messages.author: nutcracker
messages.text: I think it could be my script! Will revisit!
messages.content_html: I think it could be my script! Will revisit!

messages.author: nutcracker
messages.text: Have re-written it. The old version repeatedly opened/closed the serial connection by spawning meshcore_cli subprocesses for every receive/send (causing port contention and missed “live” events), whereas the new version maintains a single long-lived native MeshCore serial connection that continuously streams events and transmits through the same session.
messages.content_html: Have re-written it. The old version repeatedly opened/closed the serial connection by spawning meshcore_cli subprocesses for every receive/send (causing port contention and missed “live” events), whereas the new version maintains a single long-lived native MeshCore serial connection that continuously streams events and transmits through the same session.

messages.author: ChrisC
messages.text: This makes sense why your signal over here is so strong. We've got Steve (Bluetech) in Borden and myself in Bapchild (Swale). Sheppey Big Boy is now online but awaiting upgrades and that is in the docks. Back in meshtastic that was the MM3 node that had crazy coverage. We wont get the same from that unfortunately. I'm personally trying to scout a few more locations to put up stealth nodes but need to get out and about a bit more to have a look around. We've spoken about putting one up on the bridge. There are plans underway but a few of us would rather cover the area with slightly less dodgy links and use the bridge as a last resort.
